description_long: This is a modern regional turboprop airplane with a long, slender fuselage and a high-mounted straight wing configuration. The aircraft features two large propeller engines mounted on the wings, each with multiple blades, and a T-tail design with a prominent vertical stabilizer. The landing gear is visible and consists of a tricycle arrangement with two main gear units under the wings and a nose gear. The exterior is primarily white with a black tail fin that displays a red maple leaf insignia, suggesting an Air Canada livery. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the flight crew. The airplane is designed for passenger transport and is enterable, with doors and windows along the fuselage.
description: A modern regional turboprop airplane with a high-mounted straight wing, T-tail, and twin propeller engines, featuring a white body and a black tail with a red insignia.
description_view_0: Front view showing the high-mounted straight wings, twin propeller engines, and the nose of the airplane.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, cockpit windows, propeller engines, and the T-tail with the red insignia.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the T-tail configuration, twin propeller engines, and the main landing gear.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the fuselage, propeller engines, and tail design from the other side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
